body:
Update manifest and tag new patch release

```
    Updating `~/clima/ClimaAtmos.jl/.buildkite/Project.toml`
⌃ [052768ef] ↑ CUDA v5.11.2 ⇒ v5.11.3
```
title: Tag patch release

body:
## Why

The docs landing experience was a flat list of ~26 top-level pages with no grouping. Someone looking for "how do I restart a run?" or "what are the governing equations?" had to scan one long, undifferentiated menu. The getting-started pages had also drifted: duplicated install instructions, a couple of script examples that didn't match the actual `AtmosSimulation` constructor, and no single place defining the recurring `Y` / `p` / `t` notation.

The goal here is to make the docs **navigable** (group pages by what the reader is trying to do) and to make the on-ramp accurate (installation → first simulation → interfaces), so newcomers aren't misled and regulars can find things quickly.

## How to read this PR

Most of the diff is mechanical and safe to skim. To keep review focused, here's where the feedback is most appreciated:

- **`docs/make.jl` — the navigation tree.** This is the actual decision: six purpose-based
  sections (Getting Started / How-to Guides / Explanation / Reference / Developer Guide),
  with the large Explanation section split by topic. If you'd put a page elsewhere, this is
  the place to suggest so.
- **`docs/src/index.md` — the "Finding your way around" map.** The home page now guides
  readers into each section with direct links. Mainly: does it read well as a front door?

**Safe to skim (mechanical / low-risk)**

- Bibliography fixes (`ShipwayHill2012` fields; six `doi` entries that were full URLs); surfaced by build warnings.
- `tracers.md` — the ozone plot now renders via a saved PNG, clearing an oversized `@example` warning.
- Getting-started content additions: `installation.md` (REPL install, run-from-clone workflow, GPU setup), `first_simulation.md` (duration-string `dt` / `t_end`, NetCDF vs HDF5 outputs), and `single_column_prospect.md` ("Idealized cases" heading).
- `interfaces.md` — corrected the script examples to the real constructor kwargs (`setup` / `diagnostics`, not the YAML-only `initial_condition` / `default_diagnostics`); verified against `src/simulation/AtmosSimulations.jl`.
- `job_id` cleanup (`interfaces.md`, `radiative_equilibrium.md`) — it was shown as a YAML key, but is actually set via the `--job_id` flag or the constructor / `AtmosConfig` keyword argument.
- Command formatting / line-wrapping / fenced code blocks (e.g. `config_no_table.md`).
- New `glossary.md`, and removal of the un-referenced `getting_started.md` (a title-less duplicate of `installation.md`).
- `docs/Project.toml`. Adds `DocumenterInterLinks` for cross-project (`@extref`) links into the ClimaComms and Julia docs.

## Notes

- The structure draws on [Diátaxis](https://diataxis.fr) as an internal organizing lens, especially the [complex hierarchies](https://diataxis.fr/complex-hierarchies/) section.
- The site builds cleanly (doctests + render).

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)

- `first_simulation.md` still has a one-line "config-based interface" stub to fill with a short YAML example.
title: docs: reorg structure and improve getting-started
